content.summary: Sets up NDI (Network Device Interface) for receiving and sending multiple video streams over a local network in TouchDesigner. NDI In TOPs receive feeds from cameras, other TD instances, or NDI-enabled software, while NDI Out TOPs broadcast processed visuals. This technique is essential for multi-machine live production setups, LED wall driving, and distributed rendering.

codeSnippets.label: Create multi-camera NDI input setup
codeSnippets.code:
# Create multiple NDI inputs and a switcher
base = op('/project1')
camera_names = ['Camera_1', 'Camera_2', 'Camera_3']

for i, name in enumerate(camera_names):
	# Create NDI In for each camera
	ndi_in = base.create(ndiinTOP, f'ndi_cam{i+1}')
	ndi_in.par.sourcename = name
	
	# Normalize resolution
	res = base.create(resolutionTOP, f'res_cam{i+1}')
	res.par.resolutionw = 1920
	res.par.resolutionh = 1080
	res.inputConnectors[0].connect(ndi_in)

# Create switch for live cutting
switch = base.create(switchTOP, 'camera_switch')
for i in range(len(camera_names)):
	switch.inputConnectors[i].connect(op(f'res_cam{i+1}'))

# NDI output for downstream
ndi_out = base.create(ndioutTOP, 'ndi_program')
ndi_out.inputConnectors[0].connect(switch)
codeSnippets.description: Creates NDI inputs for multiple cameras, normalizes their resolution, routes through a switch, and outputs the program feed via NDI.

tips: All machines must be on the same subnet for NDI auto-discovery to work.
Use the NDI Access Manager on Windows to control which sources are visible across the network.
Set NDI In TOPs to low bandwidth mode during setup to reduce network load, then switch to full bandwidth for production.
NDI adds 1-3 frames of latency — account for this in time-critical synchronized setups.
